# elements(at:)

**Instance Method**

Returns all objects whose corresponding locations overlap the specified point.

## Declaration

```swift
func elements(at point: vector_float3) -> [ElementType]
```

## Parameters

- **point**: The point in 3D space to search.

## Return Value

An array of all matching elements, or an empty array if no objects are found.

## Discussion

You specify the point or region corresponding to an object when you add it to the tree with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKOctree/add(_:at:)] or [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKOctree/add(_:in:)] method. This method follows the same path down the tree as the two `addElement` methods, but instead of adding a new object to the tree, returns the list of all objects stored in the tree node corresponding to the specified point.

## Searching for Elements

- **elements(in:)**: Returns all objects whose corresponding locations overlap the specified volume.
